what: ocean intrusion (RTN) and sliding-law divergence (s_N) are ordered crossings of one declining normalized effective pressure n_hat toward the grounding line

identities.s_N: m/(1 - (N_c/N)^m), N = n_hat rho_i g H

prediction: along a flowline approaching the GL (n_hat falling): Type II surge band opens, then RTN=1 ocean intrusion at n_hat=1-phi, then the sliding fold (ungrounding) at n_hat_c<<1-phi. Intrusion is an upstream precursor of the sliding-instability zone.
falsification: if mapped RTN=1 lines do NOT sit inland of the high-s_N / Type-III band, or the near-flotation (low n_hat) bins are not simultaneously RTN>1 and high-s_N, the unification fails.
references: RTN: this repo §G.3/§H.1; sliding law: Schoof 2005, Joughin et al. 2019; flotation/MISI: Schoof 2007
verdict: In n_hat, RTN=(1-n_hat)/phi (intrusion at n_hat<0.10) and |s_N| diverges at n_hat_c=N_c/(rho_i g H)~0.0033 (H=2km); since n_hat_c<<1-phi, the RTN=1 intrusion line sits INLAND of the ungrounding fold for all H tested. Ocean intrusion and basal-sliding instability are the same N->0 condition, crossed in a fixed spatial order — unifying §G.3 with §G.4/§I. Committed ocean-gating terciles are consistent: near-flotation bins RTN>1 with higher TF-slope, well-grounded bins RTN<1.
